description: This guide provides a reverse-engineered account of the Apple Neural Engine (ANE), covering its architecture, programming interface, and performance characteristics across A11–A18 and M1–M5 chips. Key contributions include the first documented direct user-space dispatch path below Core ML, detailed roofline and energy-efficiency measurements (e.g., 3.8x faster and 9x more energy-efficient than GPU on 256-channel 3x3 convolution), and analysis of weight compression, firmware protocol, and cross-generation scaling. The work is based on static decompilation and on-device measurement, offering practical design rules and pitfalls for developers.
